title: Avoiding fseek in PostgreSQL
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Cursor pagination.
before_metrics: OFFSET 500000 forces the database to walk and discard hundreds of thousands of rows.
after_metrics: Composite index seek on (created_at DESC, id DESC) keeps page latency nearly flat at depth.
trade_offs: Keyset pagination is excellent for next/previous flows but does not support arbitrary page numbers without extra state.
regression_prevention: EXPLAIN test rejects large OFFSET scans; contract test inserts concurrent rows and verifies no duplicates between pages.
what_breaks_naive_approach: OFFSET gets slower with depth and drifts when concurrent inserts or deletes change earlier pages.
task: Replace deep OFFSET pagination in PostgreSQL with keyset pagination for large ordered tables.
